title: Variants passing FILTER=PASS AND DP>=30
description: Apply a compound filter: keep only variants where the FILTER column is exactly 'PASS' AND the per-sample read depth (DP in the FORMAT/SAMPLE) is at least 30. Count the survivors. Tests boolean composition of two filters.
task_prompt: You are given a VCF file at sample.vcf in your working directory. Apply BOTH of the following filters and count how many variant records survive: (1) the FILTER column (column 7) is exactly 'PASS', AND (2) the per-sample read depth is >= 30. The per-sample depth is the DP subfield of the SAMPLE1 column (the last column); the position of DP within that colon-separated value is given by the FORMAT column (column 9). Both conditions must hold. Write your answer as JSON to eval_answer.json with the schema: {"n_passing": <integer>}.
notes: PASS rows: chr1:1000 DP=30, chr1:3000 DP=45, chr2:1500 DP=60, chr2:2500 DP=22, chr3:5000 DP=50, chr3:7000 DP=80. Of these, chr2:2500 has DP=22 < 30, so 5 survive.
